title: multidevice: auto-add new device of existing group member
description:
When an existing group member connects a new device and publishes its KeyPackage, the group admin automatically adds that device's MLS leaf without requiring a manual group_add.

Why: if Alice is already in a group and connects her phone for the first time, she needs to be added to the group as a new leaf. Without this, her phone can connect to the relay but cannot decrypt any group messages. The admin must notice and act.

What to do:
- In the KEY_PACKAGE_READY broadcast handler on the admin client: check if the pub_id is already a member of any active group (check group membership table or local MLS group state)
- If yes, and if this is a new device_id not already in the group, issue a group_add for just that device's KeyPackage (single-leaf Commit + Welcome)
- Send the Welcome to the new device via whisper/deliver
- Handle the race: two admins in different groups may both try to add the new device; MLS handles duplicate adds gracefully (second Commit rejected as out-of-epoch)
- This logic runs on the client (admin side), not the relay. The relay just needs to deliver KEY_PACKAGE_READY to the admin and deliver the Welcome back.

Acceptance: Alice's second device, on connecting and publishing its KeyPackage, receives a Welcome to every group Alice is a member of within one round-trip of the admin seeing KEY_PACKAGE_READY.
